basic_details.job_title: ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 – 17 Junior Research Fellow Posts, Apply Online by 11 July 2026
basic_details.short_information: U R Rao Satellite Centre (URSC), Bengaluru - the lead satellite technology centre of ISRO under the Department of Space, Government of India - has invited online applications for 17 posts of Junior Research Fellow (JRF) vide dated 12 June 2026. The online application window is open from 12 June 2026 to 11 July 2026 at www.isro.gov.in under the "CAREERS" section. Only Indian nationals who have already passed their qualifying degree are eligible - candidates awaiting final results need not apply.

age_limit.details: The maximum age for all JRF positions is 28 years as on 11 July 2026 . Candidates wondering about age relaxation can note that SC/ST candidates get 5 years of relaxation and OBC candidates get 3 years. Additional relaxations as per Government of India orders apply to Ex-Servicemen, Persons with Benchmark Disabilities, Widows, Divorced Women, and Women judicially separated from their husbands and not remarried. | Category Age Relaxation SC / ST 5 years OBC 3 years Ex-Servicemen As per Government of India orders Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) As per Government of India orders Widows, Divorced Women, Women judicially separated (not remarried) As per Government of India orders

qualification.details: Eligibility for ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 is two-part: a qualifying postgraduate degree with the required minimum marks, and a valid national-level qualifying test score. Both conditions must be satisfied simultaneously as on the last date, i.e., 11 July 2026. Only passed candidates are eligible - students awaiting final results should not apply. | For positions JRF-E1 to JRF-E12, JRF-M1, JRF-M2, JRF-M3: M.E./M.Tech./M.Sc.(Engg.) or equivalent postgraduate degree in First Class with an aggregate minimum of 60% or CGPA/CPI of 6.5 on a 10-point scale. | For position JRF-E13 (M.Sc. route): M.Sc. in First Class with an aggregate minimum of 65% average of all semesters or CGPA/CPI of 6.84 on a 10-point scale. | For position JRF-E13 (M.Tech. route): M.Tech. or equivalent in First Class with an aggregate minimum of 60% or CGPA/CPI of 6.5 on a 10-point scale. | All degrees must be duly recognised by AICTE/UGC or any other organisation authorised by the Government of India. Essential qualifications must be completed within the stipulated duration of the course as prescribed by the university. Candidates who pursued professional courses through Open and Distance Learning (ODL) are not eligible to apply for JRF positions. | Where a university mentions both CGPA and percentage in its degree certificate or consolidated mark sheet, at least one criterion must meet DOS/ISRO eligibility norms. Conversion of CGPA into percentage of marks is not permitted to determine eligibility regardless of any conversion formula prescribed by the university. Rounding of percentage/CGPA is not allowed - values must be entered to the nearest 2 decimal places (e.g., 8.235 entered as 8.23; 8.236 as 8.24). | Candidates must have qualified one of the following national-level examinations. This is a mandatory requirement - possession of the degree alone does not make a candidate eligible to apply. | CSIR-UGC National Eligibility Test including NET-Lectureship (Assistant Professorship) GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering) conducted by MoE JAM (Joint Admission Test) conducted by MoE JEST (Joint Entrance Screening Test) National level examinations conducted by Central Government Departments and their agencies/institutions such as DST, DBT, DAE, DOS, DRDO, MoE, ICAR, ICMR, IIT, IISc, IISER, NISER, etc. | The maximum age for all JRF positions is 28 years as on 11 July 2026. Candidates wondering about age relaxation can note that SC/ST candidates get 5 years of relaxation and OBC candidates get 3 years. Additional relaxations as per Government of India orders apply to Ex-Servicemen, Persons with Benchmark Disabilities, Widows, Divorced Women, and Women judicially separated from their husbands and not remarried.

vacancy_details.Area of Work: Microcircuits Assembly & Packaging; ECAD software for layout design of Microcircuits/Hybrid Microcircuits/Multichip Modules; Advanced microelectronics packaging; Thick & Thin film processes for high density interconnection substrates

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Computer Science / Information Technology or equivalent
vacancy_details.Area of Work: User Interface development; Modelling of Mission analysis packages

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Computer Science / Information Technology or equivalent
vacancy_details.Area of Work: Development of telemetry and telecommand software packages for spacecraft operations
DC-DC converter development; 100V power bus development

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Digital Electronics / Micro-Electronics / Signal Processing / VLSI / Embedded Systems or equivalent
vacancy_details.Area of Work: Design, development and testing of spacecraft testing equipment; Analog, digital and mixed signal circuits; Micro-controller and FPGA based embedded system development; FPGA/VHDL firmware development

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Engineering Physics / Applied Optics or equivalent / Integrated M.Tech in Optical Engineering
vacancy_details.Area of Work: Development of segmented Mirror Optics; Characterization of Mirror segment; Alignment of segments to form large telescope mirror

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Applied Physics / Material Science / Material Engineering / Opto-electronics or equivalent
vacancy_details.Area of Work: THz detector material studies and characterization; Lithographic Micro-Antenna for THz/mm wavelength; THz Optics and resonant filter development

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Optical Engineering / Applied Optics / Opto-electronics or equivalent / Integrated M.Tech in Optical Engineering
vacancy_details.Area of Work: Optical subsystem design and simulation; Free space transmission studies through atmosphere for optical wavelengths; Link budget estimation and APD sensitivity studies
FPGA & ASIC development and testing; Digital signal processing; AI/ML algorithms and data analysis

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Digital Electronics / Micro Electronics / Signal Processing / Mechatronics or equivalent
vacancy_details.Area of Work: FPGA/VHDL design for digital logics, control applications, Board and System Design, integration and testing

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Digital Electronics / VLSI / Embedded Systems / VLSI and Embedded Systems or equivalent
vacancy_details.Area of Work: VLSI design, Embedded System Design and High Performance Computing Systems Design
Design, development and testing of digital circuits; Hardware Package Interface; FPGA development, MATLAB, Python, C++, LINUX Device Drivers

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.Sc in Mathematics / Physics OR M.Tech or equivalent in Aerospace
vacancy_details.Area of Work: Analysis of Flight Dynamics products (Satellite Ephemeris, eclipse prediction) for mission planning; Tracking data analysis and Orbit determination

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Thermal Engineering / Thermal Science & Engineering / Thermal Science & Energy Systems / Heat Transfer in Energy Systems or equivalent
vacancy_details.Area of Work: Numerical modelling of active thermal devices; Analysis of experimental data for AI/ML; Data driven model development; Use of MATLAB, UGNx-TMG, VC++, Python, JAVA, SQL

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Mechanical Engineering / Aerospace Engineering or equivalent
vacancy_details.Area of Work: Reliability modelling of engineering systems; Failure theories; Life Testing & Environmental Testing; Statistical distributions & Optimization; Risk Assessment

vacancy_details.Essential Qualification (Discipline): M.E/M.Tech/M.Sc(Engg.) - Mechanical Engineering / Machine Design or equivalent
vacancy_details.Area of Work: Design of mechanical elements; Stress analysis using finite element methods; Kinematic & Dynamic analysis of mechanisms; 3D modelling software tools

salary_details.details: Selected JRFs at ISRO URSC receive a monthly fellowship stipend of ₹37,000 per month. After completion of two years as JRF, at the sole discretion of ISRO/DOS/URSC, the fellow may be awarded Senior Research Fellow (SRF) status with a stipend of ₹42,000 per month. HRA at prescribed rates is paid if hostel accommodation is not provided at the place of posting. Medical benefits for self are admissible as per extant rules. No Dearness Allowance (DA) is paid. The fellowship amount is treated as basic pay for this purpose. | JRFs are eligible only for casual leave. All female JRFs are entitled to maternity leave as per Government of India instructions. JRFs are also eligible for subsidised canteen facilities. Selected candidates are not entitled to any other benefits or concessions admissible to regular employees. The initial engagement is for two years, extendable based on assessment of work and project requirements. The maximum combined period of engagement as JRF and SRF together shall not exceed five years. JRFs with aptitude for research will be encouraged to register for a Ph.D. with an external university.

selection_process: Selection for ISRO URSC JRF 2026 involves an initial screening stage followed by an interview. Meeting the minimum qualification is necessary but does not automatically entitle a candidate to be called for interview - shortlisting is based on academic performance and other parameters in the online application.

Initial Screening - Based on academic performance and other parameters given in the online application. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for interview. Interview - Conducted in Bengaluru only on a given date. Call letters for the interview will be sent by e-mail only. The interview result will be announced on the ISRO website.

URSC/ISRO reserves the right to change the venue/date of the interview. Outstation candidates shortlisted for interview will be reimbursed to-and-fro Second Class Sleeper Railway fare by the shortest distance from the address in the online application to the interview venue
or actual fare - whichever is less - on production of proof of journey. If a candidate travels by a higher class
only second class rail fare excluding reservation/sleeper charges will be paid. Travel allowance is payable only to candidates allowed for interview after successful document verification.
how_to_apply: Applications for ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 must be submitted online only. All further communications will be made exclusively by e-mail and through the ISRO website. Candidates are advised to check their e-mail regularly and visit www.isro.gov.in periodically for updates.
Visit www.isro.gov.in and navigate to the "CAREERS" section
Find Advertisement No. URSC:02:2026 and select the JRF position you wish to apply for
Register online - note your Registration Number carefully and preserve it for future reference
Fill in all required fields including a valid and correct e-mail ID - hall tickets will be sent by e-mail only

Upload the following mandatory documents in soft copy: Recent colour passport-size photograph (not older than 6 months) - JPG/JPEG, maximum 500 KB Signature - JPG/JPEG, maximum 500 KB Mark cards of each semester/year with final pass certificate - single PDF, not exceeding 1 MB SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Serviceman certificate if seeking age relaxation - PDF, not exceeding 1 MB CSIR-UGC-NET score card or GATE score card or equivalent national examination score card - PDF, not exceeding 1 MB
Recent colour passport-size photograph (not older than 6 months) - JPG/JPEG, maximum 500 KB
Mark cards of each semester/year with final pass certificate - single PDF, not exceeding 1 MB
SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Serviceman certificate if seeking age relaxation - PDF, not exceeding 1 MB
CSIR-UGC-NET score card or GATE score card or equivalent national examination score card - PDF, not exceeding 1 MB

Candidates already employed under Central/State Government, Public Sector Undertaking, or Autonomous Body must follow up the online application with a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer, indicating name, position applied for, Position No., and Registration No. on the reverse of the NOC
Avoid submitting multiple applications for the same position - if multiple applications are submitted, only the latest application submitted before the closing date will be processed
For any clarification, candidates may write to [email protected]. No interim correspondence will otherwise be entertained. Canvassing in any form will result in disqualification of candidature.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for receipt of online applications is 11 July 2026. This date is also the cut-off for determining age and qualification eligibility for all purposes.

faq.question: Q2. How many JRF posts are available in ISRO URSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 16 Junior Research Fellow positions have been notified under Advertisement No. URSC:02:2026 across Electronics, Computer Science, Optics, Mechanical, and allied disciplines. The number is provisional and may vary based on actual requirements.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for ISRO URSC JRF 2026?
faq.answer: The maximum age is 28 years as on 11 July 2026. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation and OBC candidates get 3 years. Ex-Servicemen, PwBD, and certain women categories receive relaxation as per Government of India orders.

faq.question: Q4. Is GATE or NET mandatory to apply for ISRO URSC JRF?
faq.answer: Yes, it is mandatory. Candidates must have qualified CSIR–UGC NET (including Lectureship), GATE, JAM, JEST, or a national-level examination conducted by Central Government departments or agencies such as DST, DBT, DAE, DOS, DRDO, MoE, IIT, IISc, IISER, NISER, etc. Possession of the postgraduate degree alone does not make a candidate eligible.

faq.question: Q5. What is the minimum percentage required for ISRO JRF 2026?
faq.answer: For most positions, a minimum of 60% aggregate or CGPA/CPI of 6.5 on a 10-point scale in M.E./M.Tech./M.Sc.(Engg.) is required. For JRF-E13 via the M.Sc. route, the minimum is 65% aggregate or CGPA/CPI of 6.84.

faq.question: Q6. What is the fellowship stipend for ISRO URSC JRF?
faq.answer: JRFs receive ₹37,000 per month. This may be enhanced to ₹42,000 per month upon promotion to Senior Research Fellow at the sole discretion of ISRO/DOS/URSC after two years. HRA is also paid if hostel accommodation is not provided. No DA is paid.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves two stages: an initial screening based on academic performance and application parameters, followed by an Interview conducted in Bengaluru. Call letters for the interview are sent by e-mail only. The result is published on the ISRO website.

faq.question: Q8. Can candidates awaiting final results apply for ISRO JRF 2026?
faq.answer: No. Only passed candidates are eligible to apply. Students waiting for their final results are not eligible and should not apply.

faq.question: Q9. Where should I apply for ISRO URSC JRF 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online only at www.isro.gov.in under the "CAREERS" section. No other mode of application is accepted. For clarifications, write to [email protected].

faq.question: Q10. Does the JRF position lead to a permanent job at ISRO?
faq.answer: No. The JRF position does not confer any claim or right for regular appointment in URSC or in any ISRO/Department of Space centre. The maximum combined engagement as JRF and SRF together shall not exceed five years.

title: ISRO URSC JRF Recruitment 2026 – 17 Junior Research Fellow Posts, Apply Online by 11 July 2026
